issuesFound.title: Page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Users relying on screen readers or browser tabs cannot identify the page content or context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name and aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an icon with aria-hidden="true" and no text or aria-label. It also controls a menu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 37-42:

issuesFound.title: Search button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button inside the search form contains only an image with alt="" and aria-hidden="true". It has no visible text or aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 129-131:

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image lacks an alt attribute. Even though the parent div is aria-hidden, the img tag itself should be valid HTML with alt="" if decorative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169-174:

issuesFound.title: Section missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: This is one of multiple <section> elements in the document. It lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for users to distinguish this landmark from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 151: <section class="hero" aria-label="Hero">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section references aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document (the h2 on line 195 has no id).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 195:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Link with non-descriptive text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Learn more" does not describe the destination without surrounding context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know what they are learning more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: Learn more about the scanner

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (line 239) to h5 (line 246) without intermediate h3 or h4 levels. This disrupts the logical outline for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 246:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The "Solutions" button toggles a submenu (line 86) but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 82-85: <button
  type="button"
  aria-expanded="false"
>
  Solutions
</button>

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element "Feature" is part of a header row with multiple <th> elements. It lacks a scope attribute to define its relationship to the data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 510: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users will not know what information to enter.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 733-741: <input
  type="email"
  id="newsletter-email"
  name="email"
  autocomplete="email"
  required
  aria-required="true"
  placeholder="you@example.com"
  aria-label="Email address for newsletter"
>

issuesFound.title: Form input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute, which helps users with cognitive disabilities or motor impairments fill forms faster.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 787-792: <input
  type="text"
  id="contact-first-name"
  name="first_name"
  autocomplete="given-name"
  required
>

issuesFound.title: Nav landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements in the document. This primary navigation lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 36: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring when the outline width is zero. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements relying on this global rule, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring specifically for buttons, blocking keyboard access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the mobile navigation toggle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without restating the box-shadow visible alternative in the same rule-block. While :focus has a box-shadow, strict reliance on cascade can fail in some contexts; the :focus-visible block must independently provide the indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Links inside feature cards lose their focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Filter buttons become inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Pricing toggle buttons lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible alternative. FAQ accordion buttons are not focus-visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav link :focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Footer links are inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative. Social media links lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without a visible alternative in the same block. Form inputs may lose focus indication for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px. This is below the recommended 44px target size (WCAG 2.5.8). While it meets the 24px minimum, it degrades experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size and relies on padding that may not compensate sufficiently.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input sets height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button sets min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button sets min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The rule #site-search label.sr-only sets display: none. This hides the label from screen readers, making the search input inaccessible. The .sr-only class definition (Line 565) is also broken (position: static), but this specific usage actively hides the label from AT.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 600 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform and transition for 3D rotation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 685 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override for this selector to disable the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 1.47:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector sets color: #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is 1.47:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1 for normal text).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #1e293b ratio 3.1:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p selector sets color: #94a3b8 on a dark background #1e293b. The contrast ratio is 3.1:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Many links (e.g. in footer, nav) rely solely on colour to distinguish themselves, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.

fpTitles: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1267: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1285: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the aria-controls attribute on the trigger to reference the drawer's ID. This breaks the programmatic association between the button and the region it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: if (_drawer && _drawer.id) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer (_drawer) by removing the open CSS class, but does not set aria-hidden="true". Relying solely on CSS for visibility may not remove the element from the accessibility tree in all contexts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel and updates visual classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2336: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons (_periodBtns) using CSS classes but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. These buttons act as a toggle group and require this state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2739: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function dynamically updates price text across all plan cards when the billing period changes, but does not announce this change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices updated');

issuesFound.title: productFilter grid updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function shows/hides product cards and updates the count badge based on user filter selection, but does not announce these changes to an aria-live region. Users are not informed of the new result count or state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1933: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top when activated but does not announce this action via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden change in viewport position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function dynamically counts up numbers for statistics when they enter the viewport, but does not announce the final value or the update via an aria-live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3393: liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${cfg.target}`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. This is a critical failure for form validation accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 996: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function clears the error description link when a field becomes valid but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1009: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function configures the search input with aria-controls and aria-autocomplete but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". The initial state of the suggestion list is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1596: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

fpTitles: statCounterAnimator updates not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button in SiteHeader controls the MobileNav panel via the isOpen state but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users are not informed of the panel's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 763: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu trigger button inside MobileNav controls a nested list via the isExpanded state but lacks aria-expanded. Users cannot perceive whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs toggle the active category via the activeFilter state but lack aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot identify which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button reflects the selected billing period via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed. Users cannot perceive which billing option is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1179: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button reflects the selected billing period via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed. Users cannot perceive which billing option is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1187: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not apply aria-busy to the native button element. Screen readers are not notified that the button is busy processing a request.
issuesFound.fix: Line 364: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection <section> element contains a visible heading (h1#hero-heading) but lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 803: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar <section> element contains a visible heading (h2#stats-heading) but lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav landmarks on the page (Main, Mobile, Footer), this nav is not uniquely identifiable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a <nav> element with an ID but no aria-label. It is not uniquely distinguishable from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard <article> element contains a heading (h3#headingId) but lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users cannot easily associate the article content with its title when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1233: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second <ul> in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for the marquee effect but is exposed to assistive technology. This causes duplicate content to be announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 993: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing aria-label for formatted text
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem value paragraph displays formatted text (e.g., "40,000+") but ignores the provided stat.valueLabel (e.g., "40,000 plus"). Screen readers may pronounce symbols incorrectly without the explicit label.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 877:

{stat.value}
